zh.Tyldesley, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾泰爾德斯利
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley於1290年出生在英國。她於1309年與Richard de   Tyldesley在英國藍卡爾泰爾德斯利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.Tyldesley, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾泰爾德斯利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley于1290年出生在英国。她于1309年与Richard de   Tyldesley在英国藍卡爾泰爾德斯利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tyldesley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley was born in 1290, in England. She married Richard de   Tyldesley in 1309, in Tyldesley, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley est née en 1290, à Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard de   Tyldesley en 1309, à Tyldesley, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley wurde 1290 in England geboren. Sie hat 1309 in Tyldesley, Lancashire, England Richard de   Tyldesley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley è nata il nel 1290 nel England. Lei sposato Richard de   Tyldesley nel 1309, nel Tyldesley, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Tyldesley, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアトイルデスリー
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesleyは1290年にイギリスで生まれた。1309年にイギリスランカシュアイアトイルデスリーでRichard de   Tyldesleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley(은)는 1290년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1309년 영국 랭카실르 타이데슬리에서 Richard de   Tyldesley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley nasceu em 1290, em Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Richard de   Tyldesley em 1309, em Tyldesley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley родилась в 1290 г. в местности Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de   Tyldesley в 1309 г. в местности тилдесли, ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley nació en 1290, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Richard de   Tyldesley en 1309, en Tyldesley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
